title: Prognostic Value of Pre-Treatment 18FDG-PET in Operable Breast Cancer
author: Vincent Vinh-Hung
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bf7jjrkn
materials: The breast tumor is a primary sarcoma
Surgery was done for palliation, for symptom control
Number of examined axillary lymph nodes

steps.title: Metastatic disease demonstrated by imaging modes other than FDG-PET
steps.instruction: Metastatic disease demonstrated by imaging modes other than FDG-PET

steps.title: Pattern of PET positivity (visual pathologically...
steps.instruction: Pattern of PET positivity (visual pathologically increased uptake): breast, axillary-supraclavicular region, internal mammary nodes, distant.

steps.title: Standard uptake value (SUV) based on regions of interests
steps.instruction: Standard uptake value (SUV) based on regions of interests: SUVmax global (whole body). SUVmax within breast, right and left. SUVmax axillary-supraclavicular region, right and left. SUVmax internal mammary nodes.

steps.title: Disease status at last follow-up (NED,...
steps.instruction: Disease status at last follow-up (NED, no evidence of disease/WD, with cancer)

steps.title: Descriptive statistics
steps.instruction: Descriptive statistics. Clinical- pathological characteristics and patterns of PET uptake.

steps.title: Relationships
steps.instruction: Relationships: between the characteristics and the patterns of PET uptake.

steps.title: Disease-free survival (DFS)
steps.instruction: Disease-free survival (DFS): event defined as any local-regional or distant recurrence, new primary tumor, or death from any cause.

steps.title: Overall survival (OS)
steps.instruction: Overall survival (OS): event defined as death from any cause.

steps.title: Explorative analyses
steps.instruction: Explorative analyses: Multivariate Cox regression analyses of DFS and OS. Evaluate the prognostic value of patterns of PET positivity using the Akaike information criterion (AIC) and using indexes of the proportion of variation explained by covariates.

steps.title: Handling of missing data
steps.instruction: Handling of missing data: If missing in <10% of the cases, impute using multivariate imputation by chained equations. If missing in 10% or more, consider separate analyses.
